div.p: The shell does not evaluate boolean expressions. It runs commands and checks their exit status.

ul.li: [ is just a program that exits 0 or 1
if checks: did the command exit 0?
This is why if [ $x -gt 5 ] fails when x="hello world" — the [ command receives too many arguments!

div.p: Located at: /usr/bin/[ Also symlinked as: test
• First arg is [ (command name) • Last arg must be ] (syntax) • Middle args are the expression
0 = Success/True Non-zero = Failure/False (Opposite of C boolean convention!)
Numeric: -eq equal -ne not equal -lt less than -gt greater than -le less or equal -ge greater or equal
String: = equal != not equal -z zero length -n non-zero length
File: -f exists (regular) -d exists (directory) -r readable -w writable -x executable
